events.title: Tap In Soirée — Tangier Jazz Café
events.description: Weekly Tuesday jazz night at Café de Paris, frequented by local painters, writers, and musicians. Free entry.

events.title: Grand Socco Friday Market, Tangier
events.description: The weekly market at Tangier's Grand Socco. Rif mountain villagers sell produce, herbs, and handicrafts. Best street photography moment in the city.

events.title: Grand Socco Friday Market
events.description: Weekly Friday market at Tangier's main square. Rif mountain villagers sell produce, spices, and handicrafts. Best street photo moment in Tangier.

events.title: Marrakech Biennale
events.description: Avant-garde contemporary art, literature and film across the Medina. Pop-up exhibitions, workshops, and debates. Free outdoor installations. Runs through May.

events.title: Tangier Photography Days
events.description: Annual international street photography festival with outdoor gallery along the harbour promenade. Workshops with Moroccan photographers.

events.title: Asilah Murals Festival (Warm-up exhibitions)
events.description: The annual Moussem cultural festival of Asilah opens with new mural installations along the ramparts. Perfect for your Asilah day trip this weekend.

events.title: Ramadan Night Market, Tangier
events.description: If Ramadan is underway, the souk comes completely alive after iftar sunset. Musicians, street food, and crowds until 3am. Unmissable atmosphere.

events.title: Chefchaouen Thursday Souk
events.description: Weekly market day at the bottom of Bab Souk. Local Rif farmers, weavers, and cheese-makers. Best light for photography is the morning sessions.

events.title: Eid al-Fitr Celebrations — End of Ramadan
events.description: Nationwide celebrations. Chefchaouen's blue medina fills with families in traditional dress. Dawn prayers, communal feasts, and joyful atmosphere all day.

events.title: Chefchaouen Blue Market
events.description: Regular Thursday souk in the blue city. Local artisans, Rif mountain weavers, and regional food producers fill the Plaza Uta el-Hammam.

events.title: Bou Inania Madrasa Evening Opening
events.description: The UNESCO-listed madrasa has extended evening opening hours during Islamic celebrations. Golden light illuminates the intricate tilework — the best photography window of the week.

events.title: Eid al-Fitr — End of Ramadan
events.description: Nationwide celebrations marking the end of Ramadan. Night markets, traditional music, communal prayers at dawn, and extraordinary street atmosphere particularly in Fez and Chefchaouen.

events.title: Moulay Idriss Moussem, Fez
events.description: Religious festival honouring Morocco's founding saint. Fantasia (horseback charge) around the shrine, Sufi music processions through the medina. Very local.

events.title: Sefrou Cherry Festival (Preview)
events.description: Day trip to Sefrou for the pre-festival cherry blossom season. The orchards around the old medina turn pink. A quiet version before the main June festival.

events.title: Rabat Spring Festival — Chellah Nights
events.description: Rooftop concerts inside the ancient Roman Chellah ruins on the edge of Rabat. Intimate acoustic sets with views over the storks' nests. Tickets €5-10.

events.title: Jazzablanca
events.description: Morocco's premier jazz and world music festival held in Casablanca. International jazz acts and local talent across multiple stages.

events.title: Rabat Skate Contest — Hassan II Boulevard
events.description: Recurring quarterly street skate comp organised by local crew Moroccan Skateboarders. Usually runs along the wide pavements near Tour Hassan. Great filming opportunity.

events.title: Mawazine Festival Pre-Party, Rabat
events.description: Lead-up events to Morocco's biggest music festival. Free outdoor stages begin setting up along the Rabat coast. Check the official lineup for warm-up acts.

events.title: Mawazine Festival (Rabat)
events.description: One of the world's largest music festivals — free outdoor stages, 2M attendees, international headliners + Arab superstars. The city transformed.

events.title: Friday Kasbah Oudayas Cultural Event
events.description: Regular Friday evening of traditional Andalusian music in the 17th-century Kasbah's garden. Free. Only 40-50 people, very intimate.

events.title: Essaouira Kite & Windsurf Championship
events.description: Annual kitesurfing and windsurfing championship on the Essaouira beach. Spectator-friendly with food trucks and live music on the beach.

events.title: L'Boulevard Festival — Urban Music
events.description: Morocco's biggest underground hip-hop, rap, and R&B festival in Casablanca. Discover Morocco's best new urban artists.

events.title: Mawazine Festival, Rabat
events.description: One of the world's biggest music festivals. Massive free outdoor stages along the Rabat coast. International headliners + Arab world stars. 2M+ attendees.

events.title: Casablanca Street Art Tour
events.description: Organized weekend walk through the murals of Mers Sultan and L'Abattoir. Incredible urban photography.

events.title: Essaouira Beach Bonfire & Surf Session
events.description: Recurring Saturday evening bonfire at Sidi Kaouki bay, 30km south. The vanlife + surf community gathers. Bring food to share.

events.title: Rose Festival, El Kelaa M'Gouna
events.description: The Damask rose harvest celebrated in the Dades Valley. Parades, traditional Berber music & dance, artisan markets, and rose-everything. Day trip from Marrakech.

events.title: Essaouira Kite & Windsurf Competition
events.description: Annual kitesurfing championship. Spectator beach with food trucks, live gnaoua music performance in the evening near the port.

events.title: Gnaoua Festival Warm-Up Concerts, Essaouira Port
events.description: The iconic Gnaoua festival begins. Free outdoor stages on the port. World-class mâalems (gnaoua masters) perform spiritual trance music. Goes until 3am.

events.title: Gnaoua World Music Festival — Main Weekend
events.description: The biggest night of the Gnaoua Festival. Multiple stages, fusion collaborations with international jazz and blues artists. The entire medina is a concert venue.

events.title: L'Boulevard Urban Music Festival
events.description: Underground festival showcasing Morocco's hip-hop, rap, reggae and rock scenes. Massive platform for emerging Moroccan urban artists. Expect insane energy.

events.title: Essaouira Wednesday Artisan Market
events.description: The souk outside Bab Doukkala has the best thuya wood craftsmen in Morocco. Photographers report golden-hour light at 6pm through the argan oil stalls.

events.title: Anchor Point Surf Jam
events.description: Local surf competition at the famous Anchor Point break. Great energetic vibe and community meetup.

events.title: Festival of World Sacred Music, Fez
events.description: Gathering of musicians, scholars and artists from across the world in the UNESCO-listed Fez medina. Free outdoor concerts at Bab Makina. Runs 10 days.

events.title: Rose Festival Day Trip — El Kelaa M'Gouna
events.description: Annual Damask rose harvest festival in the Dades Valley. 4h from Marrakech. Parades, Berber music, rose water market. Extremely photographic. Highly recommended as a weekend trip.

events.title: Gnaoua World Music Festival
events.description: One of Morocco's most iconic events. Spiritual Gnaoua music and trance dances take over the entire medina and port. Free outdoor stages all weekend.

events.title: Jemaa el-Fnaa Storytellers — Halqa Night
events.description: Thursday is the best night for the traditional halqa (storytelling circle) in the main square. Storytellers, snake charmers, and fire performers all compete for the crowd.

events.title: Tbourida Horses Show Rehearsal, Marrakech
events.description: A traditional Moroccan fantasia rehearsal at the hippodrome near Menara Gardens. Spectacular military horsemanship. Usually open to spectators. Free.

events.title: Marrakech Biennale — Medina Installations
events.description: Avant-garde contemporary art installations throughout the historic medina. Pop-up exhibitions in riads, museums, and courtyards. Running since March — pick the best ones.

events.title: Ourika Valley Berber Festival (Weekend)
events.description: In the Atlas foothills, the Ourika Valley hosts a smaller moussem (pilgrimage festival) in late May. Traditional Amazigh music and communal celebrations. Day trip from Marrakech.

events.title: Marrakech Marathon Weekend Street Festival
events.description: The annual marathon brings influx of international runners and the city puts on street performers, musicians, and food stalls along the route for 2 days.

events.title: Night Markets — End of Season Souk
events.description: Late-May night markets around Djemaa el-Fna extend until midnight with outdoor dining, musicians, and traditional artisans. The last big push before summer heat.
